profile.personality: enthusiastic, makes more grammar slips, talks about football a lot

transcript.content: Hi Rohan, I'm Miss Maya. I'm really glad you're here to practice English. How was your day today?

Hi Maya, nice to meet you! My day was ok, but I feel a bit shame because I made a silly mistake at work today. I said *“I will deposit the money in the market”* instead of *“market”* I meant *“bank”*. So embarrassing! 😅
Hi Rohan, you are doing great, just a tiny tweak. We say "deposit money in the *bank*," not the market. No one gets it right all the time! What happened after you said it?

Everyone just stared at me for a moment, and I could feel my face turning red. One of my colleagues laughed and said, "Are you investing in stocks now?" I had to correct my self quickly and say "bank" again. Oh man, it was so cringe! 😬
Hi Rohan, that sounds really awkward. But you handled it well! How did you feel after fixing it?

I felt a bit better after I corrected it, but I was still super embarrassed. My colleague kept making jokes about it, like "Rohan, when are you starting your stock trading career?" Honestly, it took me the whole afternoon to feel normal again. 😅 But hey, at least I learned my mistake! Do you ever mix up words like that?
Hi Rohan, I get it completely—happens to everyone! You did great staying calm. Did you talk about it with anyone else later?

No, I didn't talk to anyone else about it. I just tried to act like it never happened! But honestly, it was stuck in my head for the rest of the day. Even at lunch, I was thinking, "Why did I say *market*?" It's funny how small things can make you feel so awkward. 😅 How about you—have you ever said something wrong in front of people?
Hi Rohan, yes, I have! Once I called my boss *uncle* by mistake. Very embarrassing! How did you manage to focus after lunch?

After lunch, I just took a deep breath and tried to focus on my work. I reminded myself that everyone makes mistakes, right? But still, every time I heard someone mention "market" or "bank," I flinched a little. It was *khona*, man—so frustrating. I just want to forget the whole thing now! 😅
Hi Rohan, I understand completely—*khona* is such a strong word for that feeling. What made you want to forget it most? The jokes, the staring, or the word mix-up?

All of it, honestly! The mix-up, the staring, the jokes—it was like a *full package of shame*. I kept replaying it in my head, like, “Why didn’t I just say *bank* the first time?” But I guess it’s a good reminder to be more careful with my words. 😅 You know, I still get nervous sometimes during meetings. What do you do when you feel embarrassed?
Hi Rohan, I’m glad you see the silver lining! When I feel embarrassed, I take a deep breath and remind myself it’s just a moment. Do you talk to anyone about your nerves before meetings?

I don’t really talk to anyone about it, to be honest. I just listen to some football podcasts before meetings to calm my mind. It helps a bit, but I still get that *stomach twist* feeling. Ugh! Maybe I should try your way and just breathe more. Do you think that would work? 😅
Hi Rohan, breathing really helps—try it! What football team do you follow?

I follow Manchester United, of course! They are my favorite. Sometimes I also watch Kolkata football matches, since I love Bengali cinema and culture. Football is a good way to relax for me. Do you like football, Miss Maya? 😊
Hi Rohan, I like watching football too! Who is your favorite player in Manchester United?

My favorite player is Bruno Fernandes—he’s amazing! His energy and goals make me excited every match. I also love watching Mohun Bagan games back home, it’s like *jibon* (life) for me. Anyway, I think I should stop talking about football and focus on improving my English. Thanks for your help, Maya! I’ll try to breathe more next time I feel nervous. Talk to you soon! 😊
Hi Rohan, you’re very welcome! Talking about football is fine—we’re learning, after all! Tell me, what part of English would you like to work on next?
